# Shoda, the Last Guardian  
![](data:image/png;base64,)  

*The Bridge That Broke the Horde*

---
## Legend
Commander Shoda was a military officer of little note in the Golden Empire. He was a competent strategist and a brave warrior, but it was not until the Siege of the Imperial City—known as the Court Among the Clouds—that he passed into legend.

During the final push by the orcish hordes, one bridge remained to the inner sanctum. The rest had been destroyed to slow the assault. Shoda saw what his retreating comrades could not: if the last bridge fell too soon, hundreds would be lost. So he stayed. With his great tetsubo in hand, he faced the oncoming tide alone.

There, beneath the crumbling banners of the Empire, Shoda shattered bones and broke ranks. The bridge became a graveyard. He held for long moments, giving time for the evacuation and for the bridge to be destroyed behind him. When the orcs finally breached, they swept over him—his body was never recovered, but the orcish warband had left behind his weapon and severed head, an unspoken honor for a warrior beyond compare.

---

## Summoning Rules
- Spirit Level: 3rd
- Alignment: Lawful Neutral (LN)
- Constellation: Hero
- Binding DC: 20
### Totems
You gain a +2 totem bonus on binding checks made with Shoda if one of the following requirements is met, or a +4 bonus if two or more are met:
- You are wearing ceremonial silk armor.
- You are wielding a masterwork or magical melee weapon in hand during the summoning.
- You place a shard or token from a broken or destroyed bridge within the summoning circle.

### Ceremony
You kneel in the seal and present your weapon in salute, reciting an oath to hold the line for those who cannot defend themselves. As your voice rises, the air grows still.
### Manifestation
A thunderous shout splits the silence. A warrior clad in horned, ornate armor crashes into the seal, striking the ground with a great tetsubo. Cracks radiate outward, smoke curling from them. His mask—shaped like an ogre's sneer—hides empty eyes, and his stance radiates stoic fury.

---
## Major Granted Abilities
### I Am the Lightning That Shatters the Ground (Su)
As a standard action, you may strike the ground with a greatclub or similar two-handed bludgeoning weapon. All adjacent enemies must succeed on a Reflex save (DC 10 + ½ your Pact Maker level + your Charisma modifier) or be knocked prone. Enemies who succeed are staggered for 1d3 rounds instead. Evasion negates the effect entirely. You may use this ability once every 5 rounds.

**Capstone Empowerment:** Enemies who fail their save are also dazed for 1 round.

---

## Minor Granted Abilities

### My Enemies Are Numerous (Ex)
When you are threatened in melee by two or more enemies, you gain a +1 insight bonus to AC for each enemy beyond the first (+1 bonus for 2 enemies, +2 bonus for 3 enemies, etc). The maximum bonus is equal to the greater of your Charisma bonus and your binder level (If your Charisma Modifier is +6 and your Binder level is 5, the max bonus is +5)

### I Will Reap Vengeance Against All (Ex)
While bound to Shoda, enemies provoke an attack of opportunity from you when they take a 5-foot step within your threatened area.

### Shoda’s Knowledge(Su)
You gain proficiency and Weapon Focus with the Hammers Fighter Weapon Group. 

---
## Signs and Influence
- Physical Sign: A jagged, bloodless scar encircles your neck, marking the site of Shoda’s severed head.
- Personality Influence: When civilians or innocents are endangered, you feel compelled to stand your ground to defend them. You may not retreat if doing so would endanger unarmed noncombatants.
- Favored Allies: Humanoid (soldiers, town guards), Construct (imperial war constructs)
- Favored Enemies: Humanoid (orc), Giant (ogres, trolls)
---
## Vestigial Boon
### Guardian’s Endurance (Ex)
You gain fast healing 1 as long as you are within 10 feet of an ally.  This replaces My Enemies are Numerous.
